question_text: Which of the following correctly defines 'water pollution' as an environmental health concern that affects both human health and aquatic ecosystems?

options.text: Water pollution refers to the natural evaporation of water from rivers and lakes during summer

Water pollution is the contamination of water bodies such as rivers, lakes, groundwater and oceans by harmful substances including chemicals, sewage, industrial waste and pathogens, making the water unsafe for human use and harmful to aquatic life
Water pollution means adding chlorine to water for purification purposes in treatment plants
Water pollution refers to the natural flow of rainwater into rivers during the monsoon season

explanation: Water pollution is the contamination of water bodies (rivers, lakes, groundwater, oceans) by harmful substances — industrial effluents, sewage, agricultural chemicals (pesticides, fertilisers), plastics and pathogenic microorganisms. This contamination makes water unsafe for drinking, bathing, irrigation and recreation, and harms aquatic ecosystems.
elimination_hints: A describes evaporation (natural process); C describes purification (opposite of pollution); D describes natural runoff. Only B describes harmful contamination.
subject: Physical Education

question_text: Which of the following is a major cause of air pollution in Indian cities that directly contributes to respiratory diseases such as asthma, bronchitis and lung cancer among the urban population?

options.text: Planting more trees in urban parks and roadside areas
Vehicular emissions from cars, trucks and two-wheelers, combined with industrial smoke, construction dust, crop stubble burning and thermal power plant emissions
Increasing the number of cycling lanes and pedestrian walkways in the city
Installing solar panels on residential rooftops to generate clean energy

explanation: Major causes of air pollution in Indian cities include: vehicular exhaust (CO, NOx, PM2.5), industrial emissions, construction dust, burning of crop stubble (especially in north India), thermal power plant emissions and waste burning. These release particulate matter, toxic gases and carcinogens that cause respiratory and cardiovascular diseases.
elimination_hints: A (planting trees) and D (solar panels) reduce pollution; C (cycling lanes) reduces vehicular emissions. Only B lists causes of air pollution.
subject: Physical Education

question_text: Which of the following correctly identifies the permissible noise level limit recommended by the World Health Organization for residential areas to protect human health from the harmful effects of noise pollution?

options.text: 120 decibels (dB), which is the level of a rock concert or jet engine at close range
55 decibels (dB) during the daytime and 45 decibels (dB) during the nighttime for residential zones
0 decibels (dB), meaning complete silence at all times in residential areas
200 decibels (dB), which is beyond the range of human hearing

explanation: The WHO recommends daytime noise levels below 55 dB and nighttime levels below 45 dB for residential areas to protect health. Prolonged exposure to noise above these levels can cause hearing impairment, sleep disturbance, cardiovascular stress, reduced concentration and increased anxiety.
elimination_hints: A (120 dB) causes immediate hearing damage; C (0 dB) is impossible in any environment; D (200 dB) is a destructive shockwave level. B gives the realistic WHO residential guideline.
subject: Physical Education

question_text: Analyze the health effects of prolonged exposure to air pollution on the human respiratory system and identify which chain of physiological events most accurately describes the progression from exposure to chronic disease.

options.text: Air pollution has no effect on the respiratory system because the lungs can filter out all harmful particles regardless of their size or concentration

Inhaled pollutants such as PM2.5 penetrate deep into the lungs, cause inflammation of airways and alveoli, trigger excess mucus production, damage the lung tissue over time, reduce lung function capacity, and with chronic exposure can lead to conditions such as chronic bronchitis, emphysema (COPD) and increased risk of lung cancer
Air pollution only affects the skin and has no impact on the lungs or respiratory tract
Breathing polluted air strengthens the lungs by exposing them to challenging conditions, similar to physical training

explanation: PM2.5 and other pollutants penetrate deep into the respiratory tract, reaching the alveoli. This triggers chronic inflammation, excess mucus production (chronic bronchitis), destruction of alveolar walls (emphysema/COPD), reduced gas exchange efficiency, and cellular damage that increases lung cancer risk. The progression from acute irritation to chronic disease occurs with prolonged exposure.
elimination_hints: A denies the well-documented respiratory effects; C wrongly limits effects to skin; D makes the dangerous claim that pollution strengthens lungs. B accurately traces the pathological progression.
subject: Physical Education

question_text: Which of the following is a recognised harmful effect of noise pollution on human health that extends beyond just damage to the hearing mechanism of the ear?

options.text: Noise pollution improves sleep quality and promotes relaxation in all individuals
Prolonged exposure to noise pollution can cause hearing loss, sleep disturbance, increased blood pressure, cardiovascular stress, reduced concentration and heightened anxiety levels
Noise pollution has no health effects and is merely an aesthetic annoyance
Noise pollution only affects animals and has no impact on human health at any decibel level

explanation: Noise pollution has wide-ranging health effects beyond hearing: noise-induced hearing loss (cochlear damage), sleep disturbance, elevated blood pressure and cardiovascular stress (increased cortisol), reduced cognitive concentration and productivity, increased anxiety, irritability and mental health effects. These occur with chronic exposure above safe levels.
elimination_hints: A claims noise improves sleep (opposite is true); C trivialises documented health effects; D excludes humans entirely. B comprehensively lists the multi-system health impacts.
subject: Physical Education

question_text: Analyze the concept of 'occupational health hazards' and identify which of the following scenarios best illustrates an occupational health hazard specific to the sports and fitness industry.

options.text: A software engineer experiencing eye strain from prolonged computer use in an office
A swimming pool lifeguard developing chronic respiratory irritation from prolonged exposure to chlorine fumes in a poorly ventilated indoor pool facility
A farmer experiencing seasonal allergies from pollen exposure during the spring harvest
A student getting a common cold during the winter examination season at school

explanation: An occupational health hazard is a health risk arising directly from the conditions of one's workplace or occupation. A lifeguard developing respiratory irritation from chronic chlorine fume exposure in a poorly ventilated indoor pool is a sports-industry-specific occupational hazard — the health risk is directly linked to the workplace environment and job duties.
elimination_hints: A is an IT-sector hazard; C is environmental/agricultural; D is a common illness unrelated to occupation. Only B describes a hazard specific to the sports/fitness industry workplace.
subject: Physical Education

question_text: Evaluate the effectiveness of the following measures for preventing water pollution in a community, and identify which combination of interventions would provide the most comprehensive protection of the local water supply.

options.text: Only banning the use of soap and detergent by all households, without addressing industrial or agricultural pollution sources

A comprehensive approach including proper sewage treatment before discharge, regulation of industrial effluent discharge, controlled use of agricultural pesticides and fertilisers, community awareness about not dumping waste in water bodies, and regular water quality monitoring
Only installing water purifiers in individual homes while allowing factories to discharge untreated waste directly into rivers
Completely stopping all human use of water so that water bodies remain pristine and untouched

explanation: Effective water pollution prevention requires a comprehensive multi-pronged approach: sewage treatment plants, regulation of industrial effluent, agricultural runoff control (reduced pesticide/fertiliser use), community awareness to prevent waste dumping, and regular monitoring. Single-source interventions (A, C) are insufficient, and complete cessation of water use (D) is impractical.
elimination_hints: A addresses only one source; C permits industrial pollution; D is impractical. B addresses all major pollution sources comprehensively.
subject: Physical Education

question_text: Which of the following correctly explains how industrial activities contribute to air pollution and subsequently affect the health of communities living near industrial zones?

options.text: Industrial activities do not produce any air pollutants and have a positive effect on air quality in surrounding areas

Factories and power plants release pollutants including sulphur dioxide, nitrogen oxides, carbon monoxide, particulate matter and volatile organic compounds, which drift to nearby communities causing respiratory diseases, skin conditions, eye irritation and increased cancer risk
Industrial air pollution only affects the workers inside the factory and has no impact on surrounding communities
Industrial emissions improve air quality by releasing oxygen and reducing carbon dioxide levels

explanation: Industrial activities release multiple air pollutants: SO₂ (acid rain, respiratory irritation), NOx (smog, lung damage), CO (oxygen deprivation), particulate matter (deep lung penetration), and VOCs (carcinogenic). These pollutants disperse to nearby communities, causing respiratory diseases, skin conditions, eye irritation, neurological effects and increased cancer risk.
elimination_hints: A and D deny/reverse pollution effects; C wrongly limits impact to factory workers only. B correctly identifies pollutants and their community health effects.
subject: Physical Education

question_text: Which of the following is a commonly recognised cause of noise pollution in Indian urban areas that affects the health and well-being of city residents?

options.text: Sound of birds chirping in a quiet garden during early morning hours
Excessive honking of vehicles, loudspeaker use during festivals, construction site machinery, and industrial operations exceeding permissible noise limits
Sound of flowing water in a natural stream located in a forest area
Gentle music played at a low volume inside a private home during the evening

explanation: Common causes of noise pollution in Indian cities include: excessive vehicular honking, loudspeakers during festivals and events (often exceeding legal limits), construction machinery (concrete mixers, jackhammers, pile drivers), industrial operations, and airports. These sources exceed permissible noise levels and affect the health and well-being of urban residents.
elimination_hints: A (bird sounds), C (flowing water) and D (gentle music) are pleasant, low-level sounds. Only B lists actual noise pollution sources that exceed safe levels.
subject: Physical Education

question_text: Evaluate the role of environmental hygiene and community health practices in preventing the spread of vector-borne diseases such as malaria and dengue in tropical regions of India.

options.text: Environmental hygiene has no role in preventing vector-borne diseases because these diseases are purely genetic

Maintaining environmental hygiene — eliminating stagnant water where mosquitoes breed, proper waste disposal, drainage maintenance, use of mosquito nets and repellents, community fogging drives and public awareness campaigns — collectively reduces mosquito populations and human-mosquito contact, significantly lowering disease transmission
Vector-borne diseases can only be prevented by staying indoors permanently and never going outside
Environmental hygiene is relevant only for waterborne diseases and has no effect on mosquito-borne infections

explanation: Environmental hygiene is crucial for vector-borne disease prevention: eliminating stagnant water (mosquito breeding sites), proper waste disposal, maintaining drainage, using personal protection (nets, repellents), community fogging and public awareness collectively reduce mosquito populations and human-vector contact, significantly decreasing malaria and dengue transmission.
elimination_hints: A wrongly claims genetic causation; C is impractical; D incorrectly limits environmental hygiene to waterborne diseases only. B comprehensively describes the environmental approach to vector control.
subject: Physical Education

question_text: Match the following types of environmental pollution in Column A with their specific health effects on the human body in Column B.

column_b.text: Permanent hearing loss (cochlear damage), tinnitus, sleep disturbance and cardiovascular stress
Respiratory diseases (asthma, bronchitis, COPD), lung cancer and cardiovascular disease
Bioaccumulation of toxins in food chain, liver and kidney damage, neurological effects
Waterborne diseases (cholera, typhoid), heavy metal poisoning, gastrointestinal disorders

explanation: Air pollution → respiratory diseases, lung cancer, CVD (Q). Water pollution → waterborne diseases, heavy metal poisoning (S). Noise pollution → hearing loss, tinnitus, sleep disturbance (P). Soil pollution → bioaccumulation, liver/kidney damage (R).
elimination_hints: Air pollution (1) clearly relates to respiratory/lung diseases (Q); noise pollution (3) clearly relates to hearing loss (P). Anchor with these.
subject: Physical Education

question_text: Match the following occupational health hazards in Column A with the specific occupation or work environment where they are most commonly encountered in Column B.

column_a.text: Silicosis (lung disease from inhaling fine silica dust)
Heat stroke and dehydration from prolonged sun exposure
Repetitive strain injury (RSI) from continuous keyboard use
Noise-induced hearing loss from exposure to loud machinery

column_b.text: Construction workers and outdoor sports athletes training in extreme summer heat
Mining and stone-crushing industry workers exposed to rock dust
Factory workers operating heavy machinery without ear protection
Office workers and data entry professionals using computers for extended hours

explanation: Silicosis → mining/stone-crushing workers (Q). Heat stroke → construction/outdoor sports workers (P). RSI → office/data entry workers (S). Noise-induced hearing loss → factory/machinery workers (R).
elimination_hints: Silicosis (1) is the 'miner's disease' from rock dust (Q); RSI (3) is the 'computer worker's disease' (S). Anchor with these.
subject: Physical Education

question_text: Match the following preventive measures in Column A with the specific type of environmental pollution they are designed to reduce in Column B.

column_a.text: Using CNG (Compressed Natural Gas) vehicles instead of diesel vehicles
Installing sound barriers along highways near residential areas
Building sewage treatment plants before discharging waste into rivers
Using organic farming methods and reducing chemical pesticide application

explanation: CNG vehicles → reduces vehicular emissions = air pollution prevention (S). Sound barriers → blocks highway noise = noise pollution prevention (P). Sewage treatment → prevents untreated waste in rivers = water pollution prevention (Q). Organic farming → reduces chemical pesticides in soil = soil pollution prevention (R).
elimination_hints: CNG vehicles (1) clearly address air quality (S); sound barriers (2) clearly address noise (P). Use these two anchors.
subject: Physical Education

question_text: Read the Assertion and Reason below and select the correct option regarding the effects of water pollution on human health.
assertion: Communities that depend on polluted river water for drinking and cooking face a significantly higher risk of waterborne diseases such as cholera, typhoid and hepatitis A.
reason: Polluted river water contains pathogenic bacteria, viruses and parasites from untreated sewage and agricultural runoff that enter the human body through ingestion and cause gastrointestinal infections.

options.text: Both Assertion and Reason are true, and Reason is the correct explanation of Assertion
Both Assertion and Reason are true, but Reason is NOT the correct explanation of Assertion
Assertion is true but Reason is false
Assertion is false but Reason is true

explanation: The Assertion is true — polluted water increases waterborne disease risk. The Reason is also true and correctly explains why: sewage and agricultural runoff introduce pathogens into the water, and ingestion of these pathogens causes the diseases mentioned.
elimination_hints: Both statements are established public health facts. The Reason directly explains the mechanism (pathogen contamination → ingestion → infection) behind the Assertion's disease risk.
subject: Physical Education

question_text: Read the Assertion and Reason below and select the correct option regarding noise pollution and its effects on sports performance.
assertion: Excessive noise pollution in the environment surrounding a sports training facility can negatively affect an athlete's concentration, reaction time and overall training quality.
reason: Noise pollution causes only cosmetic skin problems and has no effect on the nervous system, cognitive function or psychological state of an individual.

options.text: Both Assertion and Reason are true, and Reason is the correct explanation of Assertion
Both Assertion and Reason are true, but Reason is NOT the correct explanation of Assertion
Assertion is true but Reason is false
Assertion is false but Reason is true

explanation: The Assertion is true — noise pollution can disrupt concentration, impair reaction time, increase stress and reduce training quality for athletes. However, the Reason is false — noise pollution affects the nervous system (stress response, cortisol elevation), cognitive function (reduced concentration, impaired memory) and psychological state (anxiety, irritability), not just skin. The Reason claims it causes only skin problems, which is incorrect.
elimination_hints: The Reason claims noise causes 'only cosmetic skin problems' — this is clearly false. Noise primarily affects hearing, the nervous system and cognition, not skin.
subject: Physical Education

question_text: Read the Assertion and Reason below and select the correct option regarding air pollution prevention.
assertion: Promoting the use of public transport, electric vehicles and cycling can significantly reduce air pollution levels in Indian cities.
reason: These alternatives reduce the number of fossil-fuel-burning vehicles on the road, thereby decreasing emissions of carbon monoxide, nitrogen oxides, sulphur dioxide and particulate matter that cause air pollution.

options.text: Both Assertion and Reason are true, and Reason is the correct explanation of Assertion
Both Assertion and Reason are true, but Reason is NOT the correct explanation of Assertion
Assertion is true but Reason is false
Assertion is false but Reason is true

explanation: The Assertion is true — public transport, EVs and cycling reduce air pollution. The Reason is also true and correctly explains the mechanism: these alternatives reduce fossil-fuel vehicle numbers on roads, directly decreasing emissions of CO, NOx, SO₂ and PM that constitute vehicular air pollution.
elimination_hints: Both statements are logically connected and factually correct. The Reason provides the precise emission-reduction mechanism that explains the Assertion's pollution reduction claim.
subject: Physical Education

question_text: Arrange the following steps in the correct logical order for a community-based approach to addressing a sudden increase in waterborne disease cases in a village after monsoon flooding.
items: I. Implement long-term solutions such as building proper drainage, sewage treatment and protected water supply systems
II. Conduct emergency water testing to identify the source and type of contamination
III. Provide immediate relief — distribute safe drinking water, chlorine tablets and ORS packets to affected families
IV. Launch a community Health Education campaign about water purification, handwashing and sanitation practices

options.text: III → II → IV → I
I → IV → II → III
II → III → I → IV
IV → I → III → II

explanation: Emergency response order: Immediate relief — safe water/ORS (III, save lives first) → Water testing to identify contamination (II, find the cause) → Health Education campaign for community (IV, prevent further cases through behaviour change) → Long-term infrastructure solutions (I, permanent fix). This follows the standard emergency → investigation → education → infrastructure sequence.
elimination_hints: Immediate relief (III) must come first to save lives; long-term infrastructure (I) is the last step. Anchor with these endpoints.
subject: Physical Education

question_text: Arrange the following sources of air pollution in order of their typical contribution to overall urban air pollution in major Indian cities, from the largest contributor to the smallest contributor.
items: I. Vehicular emissions (cars, trucks, two-wheelers, buses)
II. Domestic cooking fires using biomass fuels (wood, dung, crop residues)
III. Construction dust from building and road projects
IV. Industrial emissions from factories and power plants

options.text: I → IV → III → II
II → III → IV → I
III → I → II → IV
IV → II → I → III

explanation: In major Indian cities, vehicular emissions (I) are typically the largest contributor to air pollution (30-40%), followed by industrial emissions (IV, 20-30%), construction dust (III, 10-15%), and domestic cooking fires (II, lower in urban areas but significant in peri-urban/rural areas). The exact proportions vary by city but this ordering is generally accepted for urban India.
elimination_hints: Vehicular emissions (I) are the largest urban air pollution source in India — this is widely reported. Domestic cooking (II) is more significant in rural than urban areas, making it the smallest urban contributor.
subject: Physical Education

question_text: True or False: Noise pollution refers to excessive or harmful levels of noise in the environment that can cause hearing damage, sleep disturbance, stress and other adverse health effects in humans.

options.text: True
False

explanation: This statement is True. Noise pollution is defined as excessive or harmful levels of noise that disturb the natural environment and adversely affect human health — causing hearing damage, sleep disturbance, stress, cardiovascular effects and reduced quality of life.
elimination_hints: This is a standard definition of noise pollution covering both the cause (excessive noise) and effects (hearing damage, sleep disturbance, stress). All elements are correct.
subject: Physical Education

question_text: Study the diagram below that shows the three major types of environmental pollution (air, water, noise), their primary sources, health effects, and prevention methods. Based on the diagram, which type of pollution has the widest range of health effects spanning respiratory, cardiovascular, neurological and carcinogenic (cancer-causing) outcomes?

options.text: Noise pollution — because it causes respiratory diseases and lung cancer in addition to hearing loss
Water pollution — because it only causes skin rashes and has no internal organ effects

Air pollution — because inhaled pollutants affect the lungs (respiratory), heart (cardiovascular), brain (neurological via PM2.5 crossing the blood-brain barrier), and contain carcinogens that increase cancer risk across multiple organs
All three types cause identical health effects with no differences between them

explanation: Air pollution has the widest range of health effects: respiratory (asthma, bronchitis, COPD), cardiovascular (heart attacks, stroke), neurological (cognitive decline, PM2.5 crossing blood-brain barrier), and carcinogenic (lung cancer, bladder cancer). No other single pollution type spans all four categories as comprehensively as air pollution.
elimination_hints: A wrongly attributes lung cancer to noise; B underestimates water pollution effects; D ignores documented differences. C correctly identifies air pollution's uniquely broad multi-system impact.
subject: Physical Education
